html:
[201]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 已于是载威徳 - A phrase indicating that the speaker has already demonstrated their power and virtue.
  • 畧驻车徒 - Means 'briefly halted the troops,' suggesting a temporary pause in military or official activities.
  • 重念云轩既发于蓬莱 - A metaphorical expression where '云轩' refers to a cloud chariot, symbolizing a journey from the mythical island of Penglai, which is often associated with immortals.
  • 驭可游于天竺 - Indicates that the speaker can travel to Tianzhu (ancient India) with ease, suggesting a divine or supernatural ability.
  • 元印法以传心 - Means 'transmitting the Dharma through the seal of the mind,' referring to the transmission of Buddhist teachings.
  • 老氏西行 - Refers to Laozi's legendary western journey, where he is said to have left behind the *Tao Te Ching*.
  • 或化人而成道 - Means 'or transforming people into the path of enlightenment,' indicating a process of spiritual transformation.
  • 顾川途之虽濶 - Means 'regarding the vast distance of the journey,' emphasizing the long and challenging nature of the journey.
  • 瞻几杖以非遥 - Means 'the distance is not far when looking at the staff and cane,' suggesting that the goal is within reach.
  • 爰荅来章 - Means 'responding to the incoming letter,' indicating a reply to a communication.
  • 可明朕意 - Means 'to clarify my intention,' expressing the desire for the recipient to understand the sender's purpose.
  • 秋暑师比平安好防不多 - A phrase indicating that the teacher is safe and well during the autumn heat, with no major concerns.
  • 及十四日辛巳十一月至赛玛尔堪城 - Specifies the date and location of arrival, using the traditional Chinese calendar system.
  • 壬午三月过鐡门闗 - Indicates the month and year of passing through a specific pass, again using the traditional Chinese calendar.
  • 四月逹行在所 - Means 'reaching the imperial court in April,' indicating the arrival at the emperor's residence.
  • 时上在雪山之阳 - Means 'at that time, the emperor was in the south of the snowy mountains,' providing the location of the emperor.
  • 劳曰他国徴聘皆不应 - Means 'the emperor said that he had not responded to invitations from other countries,' indicating a refusal to engage with foreign powers.
  • 今逺逾万里而来朕甚嘉焉 - Means 'now coming from afar, I am very pleased,' expressing the emperor's appreciation for the visitor's long journey.
  • 赐坐就食设二帐于御幄之东以居之 - Means 'granting a seat, providing food, and setting up two tents to the east of the imperial tent for accommodation,' describing the hospitality extended to the visitor.
  • 约日问道以回纥叛亲征不果至 - Means 'arranging a date to ask questions about the Way, but the campaign against the Uighur rebellion did not materialize,' indicating a planned but unfulfilled meeting.
  • 九月设庭燎虚前席延问至道真人 - Means 'in September, setting up a court fire, clearing the front seat, and inviting the true sage of the Way,' describing a formal and respectful invitation.
  • 大略荅以节欲保躬 - Means 'responding roughly by advocating self-restraint and preserving one's body,' indicating the main points of the conversation.
  • 天道好生恶杀 - Means 'the heavenly way favors life and dislikes killing,' reflecting a moral principle.
  • 治尚无为清净之理 - Means 'governance should follow the principle of non-action and purity,' indicating a preference for minimal intervention and simplicity.
  • 上説命左史书诸防 - Means 'the emperor was pleased and ordered the Left Historian to record the conversation,' indicating the importance of the meeting.
  • 癸未乞东还 - Means 'on the day of Guiwei, requesting to return east,' indicating a request to leave.
  • 赐号神仙爵大宗师 - Means 'bestowing the title of Immortal, Grand Master, and Supreme Teacher,' indicating the high honor bestowed upon the visitor.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 蓬莱 - A mythical island in Chinese mythology, often associated with immortals and longevity.
  • 天竺 - Ancient name for India, a significant center of Buddhism.
  • 元印法 - Refers to the seal of the mind, a concept in Buddhism related to the transmission of the Dharma.
  • 老氏 - Laozi, the legendary founder of Daoism.
  • 回纥 - An ancient Turkic people who played a significant role in Chinese history.
  • 雪山之阳 - Refers to the southern side of the snowy mountains, likely the Himalayas.
  • 庭燎 - A ritual fire lit in the imperial court, symbolizing the presence of the emperor.
  • 左史 - The Left Historian, a position responsible for recording important events and conversations.
  • 神仙 - Immortals, beings who have achieved a state of eternal life.
  • 大宗师 - Grand Master, a title given to highly respected teachers or spiritual leaders.

Proper Nouns

  • 蓬莱 - The mythical island of Penglai.
  • 天竺 - Ancient India.
  • 元印法 - The seal of the mind.
  • 老氏 - Laozi.
  • 回纥 - The Uighur people.
  • 雪山之阳 - The southern side of the snowy mountains.
  • 庭燎 - The ritual fire in the imperial court.
  • 左史 - The Left Historian.
  • 神仙 - Immortals.
  • 大宗师 - Grand Master.

[202]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 掌管天下道教 - Refers to the leadership or administration of Daoism.
  • 甲申三月至燕八月 - Refers to a specific time period, likely from the lunar calendar, with '甲申' being a year in the sexagenary cycle and '燕' referring to Yan (a region or state).
  • 奉防居太极宫 - 'Fengfang' likely refers to a ceremonial or protective role, '太极宫' is the Taiji Palace, a significant Daoist temple.
  • 特改太极为长春 - '特改' means 'especially changed,' indicating a specific alteration.
  • 留颂而逝 - '留颂' means 'left a eulogy' or 'left a poem,' '逝' means 'passed away.'
  • 年八十 - 'Age eighty,' indicating the lifespan of the subject.
  • 至元己巳正月防诏 - '至元' is a reign title, '己巳' is a year in the sexagenary cycle, '正月' is the first month of the lunar year, '防诏' likely refers to an imperial edict.
  • 赠五祖七真徽号 - '赠' means 'bestowed,' '五祖七真' refers to the Five Ancestors and Seven Truths, '徽号' means 'honorific title.'
  • 曰长春演道主教真人 - '长春' is a name or title, '演道' means 'expounding the Dao,' '主教' means 'head priest,' '真人' means 'true person' or 'immortal.'
  • 初真人自行在归道由宣徳 - '初真人' refers to the subject initially, '自行在归道' means 'returned on his own accord,' '宣徳' is a place or title.
  • 日一富家新居落成 - '日一富家' means 'a wealthy family,' '新居落成' means 'the new house was completed.'
  • 礼致下顾 - '礼致' means 'invited with propriety,' '下顾' means 'to look down upon' or 'to visit.'
  • 将冀一言以为福 - '将冀' means 'hoping,' '一言' means 'a word,' '以为福' means 'to bring good fortune.'
  • 既入其室黙然无语 - '既入其室' means 'after entering the room,' '黙然无语' means 'silently without speaking.'
  • 輙以所持鐡拄杖于窓戸墙壁上颇毁数处 - '輙以' means 'then with,' '所持鐡拄杖' means 'the iron cane he held,' '于窓戸墙壁上' means 'on the windows and walls,' '颇毁数处' means 'destroyed several places.'
  • 主人再拜希解悟 - '主人' means 'the host,' '再拜' means 'bowed twice,' '希解悟' means 'hoped for understanding.'
  • 尔屋完矣美矣完而必毁理执然也 - '尔屋完矣美矣' means 'your house is complete and beautiful,' '完而必毁' means 'but it must be destroyed,' '理执然也' means 'this is the way of things.'
  • 吾不尔毁尔将无以图厥终 - '吾不尔毁' means 'I will not destroy it,' '尔将无以图厥终' means 'you will not be able to plan for the end.'
  • 今毁矣尔宜思其毁而欲完克保全之 - '今毁矣' means 'now it is destroyed,' '尔宜思其毁而欲完克保全之' means 'you should think about its destruction and strive to preserve it.'
  • 则尔与尔子子孙孙庶几歌斯哭斯永终弗替 - '则尔与尔子子孙孙' means 'then you and your descendants,' '庶几歌斯哭斯' means 'perhaps they will sing and cry,' '永终弗替' means 'forever without replacement.'
  • 主人説服吁真人真知道哉 - '主人説服' means 'the host was convinced,' '吁真人真知道哉' means 'ah, the true person truly knows.'
  • 南池鼃 - '南池' means 'South Pool,' '鼃' means 'frog.'
  • 宋季城信州掘土处为濠百畆许 - '宋季' means 'late Song dynasty,' '城信州' means 'the city of Xinzhou,' '掘土处为濠' means 'a place where earth was dug to create a moat,' '百畆许' means 'about a hundred acres.'
  • 在郡南曰南池 - '在郡南' means 'in the south of the county,' '曰南池' means 'called South Pool.'
  • 池之旁可居旧为里人屋 - '池之旁' means 'beside the pool,' '可居' means 'suitable for living,' '旧为里人屋' means 'formerly belonged to the villagers' houses.'
  • 归附后逹噜噶齐玛察据有其地 - '归附后' means 'after submission,' '逹噜噶齐玛察' is a name, '据有其地' means 'took possession of the land.'
  • 每春夏之交羣鼃聒耳寝食不安 - '每春夏之交' means 'every spring and summer,' '羣鼃聒耳' means 'the frogs' noise disturbed the ears,' '寝食不安' means 'sleep and eat uneasily.'
  • 会三十八代天师张广微【与材】朝京回 - '会' means 'happened to meet,' '三十八代天师' means 'the 38th generation Celestial Master,' '张广微' is a name, '【与材】' is a note or annotation, '朝京回' means 'returned from the capital.'
  • 因以告天师朱书符新瓦上 - '因以告天师' means 'then told the Celestial Master,' '朱书符' means 'red ink talisman,' '新瓦上' means 'placed on new tiles.'
  • 自是至今寂然 - '自是至今' means 'from then on until now,' '寂然' means 'quietly.'
  • 鴈子 - '鴈子' means 'gander.'
  • 汉书太液池中鳬雏鴈子布满充积 - '汉书' is the *Book of Han*, '太液池' is Taiye Pool, '鳬雏鴈子' means 'ducklings and ganders,' '布满充积' means 'were abundant and accumulated.'
  • 用鴈子甚佳 - '用鴈子' means 'using ganders,' '甚佳' means 'very good.'
  • 王维诗芦笋穿荷叶菱花罥鴈儿又新 - '王维诗' means 'Wang Wei's poem,' '芦笋穿荷叶' means 'reed shoots piercing through lotus leaves,' '菱花罥鴈儿' means 'water lilies trapping ganders,' '又新' means 'and new.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 掌管天下道教 - Refers to the leadership of Daoism, a major religion in China.
  • 甲申三月至燕八月 - Refers to a specific time period, likely from the lunar calendar, with '甲申' being a year in the sexagenary cycle and '燕' referring to Yan (a region or state).
  • 太极宫 - The Taiji Palace, a significant Daoist temple.
  • 五祖七真 - Refers to the Five Ancestors and Seven Truths in Daoism.
  • 长春演道主教真人 - '长春' is a name or title, '演道' means 'expounding the Dao,' '主教' means 'head priest,' '真人' means 'true person' or 'immortal.'
  • 宣徳 - A place or title, likely referring to a region or administrative division.
  • 至元 - A reign title, likely referring to the Yuan dynasty.
  • 宋季 - Refers to the late Song dynasty.
  • 信州 - A city in Jiangxi province.
  • 三十八代天师张广微 - '三十八代天师' means 'the 38th generation Celestial Master,' '张广微' is a name.
  • 汉书 - The *Book of Han*, a historical text.

[203]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 趂办官钱 - A compound term referring to the practice of raising funds through the exploitation of public officials.
  • 侵用官钱 - Means 'misappropriation of public funds.'
  • 无可为偿 - Means 'unable to repay.'
  • 无可为计 - Means 'having no other options.'
  • 命小舟载之求食 - Means 'to order boats to carry them to beg for food.'
  • 赀纳官 - Means 'to pay off debts through money.'
  • 不肖辈 - Refers to unworthy or disreputable individuals.
  • 荡浆 - Means 'to paddle a boat.'
  • 绿惨红愁 - A compound term meaning 'green and red, sorrowful and sad,' describing a state of distress.
  • 羞不语 - Means 'ashamed and silent.'
  • 低回忍泪 - Means 'to hesitate and hold back tears.'
  • 傍郎船 - Means 'to approach the boat of the man.'
  • 强歌舞 - Means 'to force oneself to dance and sing.'
  • 玉壶美酒 - Refers to fine wine.
  • 鱼腹熊蹯 - Refers to delicacies like fish and bear paws.
  • 阳台梦短 - Refers to a fleeting dream, often used to describe a short-lived affair.
  • 鸳鎻生寒 - Means 'the lock of the swan (a metaphor for a couple) feels cold,' implying a sense of loneliness.
  • 愁日暮 - Means 'to worry about the end of the day.'
  • 掷千金 - Means 'to spend a lot of money.'
  • 桑濮歌行路 - Refers to songs sung by prostitutes on the road.
  • 后王之子孙有为娼者 - Means 'later generations of the descendants of the king became prostitutes.'
  • 天之报施一何捷也 - Means 'how quickly heaven rewards or punishes.'
  • 鼎作牛鸣 - Refers to a story about a bronze vessel making a sound like a cow.
  • 比旧藏皆不能及 - Means 'even the old collection cannot match it.'
  • 颇可怪 - Means 'quite strange.'
  • 罄其所藏 - Means 'to empty all his collection.'
  • 凿深窖以埋之 - Means 'to dig a deep pit to bury it.'
  • 彛亦在列 - Means 'the bronze vessel was also among them.'
  • 既入窖作牛鸣者七夜 - Means 'after being buried, it made a sound like a cow for seven nights.'
  • 取出寄田家 - Means 'to take it out and leave it with a farmer.'
  • 其窖后遭发掘 - Means 'the pit was later excavated.'
  • 独此彛获存 - Means 'only this bronze vessel remained.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 广济库 - A government treasury in Zhejiang province during the late Yuan dynasty.
  • 府判王某 - A magistrate known for his harsh methods.
  • 鲜于伯几先生【枢】 - Xian Yu Boji, a poet who lived during the late Yuan dynasty.
  • 湖边曲 - A poem that reflects the social conditions of the time.
  • 商彛 - Bronze vessels used in ancient Chinese rituals, often considered valuable artifacts.
  • 至正壬辰 - The year 1352, a period of political instability and rebellion.

Proper Nouns

  • 广济库 - The name of the government treasury.
  • 杭城諲实戸 - The name of the households designated to serve as treasurers.
  • 鲜于伯几先生【枢】 - The name of the poet Xian Yu Boji.
  • 湖边曲 - The title of the poem.
  • 义兴王子明 - The name of the wealthy man Wang Ziming.
  • 汴夹谷郎中 - The name of the merchant from Bianjing.
  • 商彛 - The name of the bronze vessel.
  • 至正壬辰 - The year 1352.
  • 蕲黄 - The region of Qichang (modern-day Huanggang, Hubei).

[204]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 鏖糟 - Colloquial term for filth or disorder; used to describe the chaotic aftermath of a battle.
  • 俗语以不洁为鏖糟 - Colloquial saying that equates filth with disorder.
  • 按霍去病传鏖皋兰下注以世俗谓尽死杀人为鏖糟 - Reference to the *Biography of Huo Qubing* in the *Records of the Grand Historian*, where '鏖糟' describes the aftermath of a battle where all combatants were killed.
  • 然义虽不同却有所出 - Despite different meanings, the term has its origins in historical usage.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 霍去病 - Famous Han dynasty general known for his military prowess and victories against the Xiongnu.
  • 至正甲午 - The year 1344 during the reign of Emperor Huizong of the Yuan dynasty.
  • 绍兴 - A city in Zhejiang province, known for its historical significance and cultural heritage.
  • 录事司 - An administrative office responsible for recording and managing official documents.
  • 达噜噶齐 - A title for a high-ranking official in the Yuan dynasty.
  • 左丞 - A title for a high-ranking official in the Yuan dynasty.
  • 杨谔勒哲 - A high-ranking official in the Yuan dynasty.
  • 露布 - A type of official announcement or proclamation.
  • 统洞蛮 - A group of people, possibly referring to local tribes or rebels.
  • 集庆 - A city in Jiangsu province, known for its strategic importance.
  • 江南行台 - An administrative body in the southern regions of China during the Yuan dynasty.

Proper Nouns

  • 霍去病 - Huo Qubing, a famous Han dynasty general.
  • 至正甲午 - The year 1344.
  • 绍兴 - Shaoxing, a city in Zhejiang province.
  • 录事司 - A government office.
  • 达噜噶齐 - A high-ranking official title.
  • 左丞 - A high-ranking official title.
  • 杨谔勒哲 - A high-ranking official.
  • 露布 - An official announcement.
  • 统洞蛮 - A group of people.
  • 集庆 - A city in Jiangsu province.
  • 江南行台 - An administrative body.

[205]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 果毅 - A compound term meaning 'resolute and courageous,' often used as a title or epithet for military units or individuals.
  • 分拨守要害 - Literally 'distribute and guard strategic points,' indicating the deployment of troops to key locations.
  • 击鲜饫醲 - A metaphor for indulging in fine food and drink, where '击鲜' means to feast on fresh delicacies, and '饫醲' means to satisfy oneself with rich food and drink.
  • 酣咏呌啸 - Describes a state of deep enjoyment and revelry, with '酣咏' meaning to drink deeply and sing, and '呌啸' meaning to shout or roar in joy.
  • 永康防起 - Refers to a rebellion or uprising in Yongkang, a place name.
  • 旌其功 - Means 'to honor his achievements' or 'to commend his merit.'
  • 御史大夫拜珠克任奸吏为爪牙 - Refers to a high-ranking official (御史大夫) who appointed corrupt officials as his enforcers, where '拜珠克' is a proper name, '奸吏' means 'corrupt officials,' and '爪牙' means 'enforcers.'
  • 自统军三千曰台军 - Means 'he personally led three thousand troops called the '台军,' where '台军' is a proper name for a military unit.
  • 纪律不严 - Means 'lax discipline.'
  • 民横被扰害 - Means 'the people were arbitrarily harassed and harmed.'
  • 辄抑之 - Means 'would suppress them.'
  • 众军皆怨怒 - Means 'the troops all resented and were angry.'
  • 惟以钩距致财为务 - Means 'he only cared about accumulating wealth through cunning methods,' where '钩距' means 'cunning methods' or 'tricks.'
  • 不礼之 - Means 'did not show respect to him.'
  • 安问其他 - Means 'why bother with anything else?'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 果毅军 - A military unit known for its resolute and courageous nature, likely a title or name given to a specific military force.
  • 江南浙西道 - A regional administrative division during the Yuan Dynasty, covering parts of modern-day Jiangsu and Zhejiang provinces.
  • 江东建康道 - Another regional administrative division, likely referring to the area around Nanjing (建康).
  • 浙省 - Refers to the Zhejiang province, a major region in southeastern China.
  • 丞相 - A high-ranking official in the imperial government, equivalent to a prime minister.
  • 达实特穆尔 - A proper name, likely referring to a historical figure who held a high-ranking position.
  • 御史大夫 - A high-ranking official responsible for overseeing government administration and investigating corruption.
  • 拜珠克 - A proper name, likely referring to a historical figure who held a high-ranking position.
  • 台军 - A military unit, possibly a personal or regional army under the command of a high-ranking official.

Proper Nouns

  • 果毅 - A title or epithet for a military unit or individual.
  • 永康 - A place name, likely referring to a county or city.
  • 江南浙西道 - A regional administrative division.
  • 江东建康道 - Another regional administrative division.
  • 浙省 - Refers to the Zhejiang province.
  • 达实特穆尔 - A proper name, likely referring to a historical figure.
  • 拜珠克 - A proper name, likely referring to a historical figure.
  • 埒克戬永安 - A proper name, likely referring to a historical figure.
  • 阎塔斯布哈 - A proper name, likely referring to a historical figure.
  • 王哈喇 - A proper name, likely referring to a historical figure.

[206]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 謀殺之未得間 - Failed in their plot to kill him.
  • 首事出兵 - The rebels led an army out.
  • 逾曹娥江 - Crossed over the Ca'e River.
  • 與平章方國珍部下萬戸冯某鬬 - Fought against the general Fang Guozhen's subordinate, Wanhu Feng.
  • 既不利 - They were not successful.
  • 駐軍東闗 - They stationed their army at Dongguan.
  • 單騎驰归 - He rode back alone.
  • 拜意決矣 - Bai's intention was decided.
  • 遲明召君私第议事 - He summoned the governor to his private residence early in the morning to discuss matters.
  • 左右以鐡槌挝杀之 - His attendants killed him with iron hammers.
  • 初甚秘守 - Initially it was kept very secret.
  • 守阍军自相谓无已杀总督官我辈幸也 - The guards at the gate told each other that they were glad that the governor had been killed.
  • 民始有闻之者 - The people began to hear about it.
  • 走白君部将 - They rushed to inform the governor's generals.
  • 浙东佥元帅黄中 - The deputy commander of the Zhejiang East region, Huang Zhong.
  • 诸叅谋闻变奔避 - The various advisors heard the news and fled.
  • 至有坠城以出行四五十里者 - Some even fell off the walls and traveled forty or fifty miles away.
  • 初夜二鼔 - Early in the night, the second drumbeat.
  • 中提军入城屯戒珠山 - Zhong led his troops into the city and camped at Jiezhu Mountain.
  • 拜未及知中卧病方饮药得少汗尚昏溃困顿 - Bai did not know about this because he was ill in bed, just having taken medicine and sweating slightly, still in a confused and exhausted state.
  • 左右扶翼擐甲上马 - His attendants supported and helped him put on armor and mount his horse.
  • 遇台军于江桥鬬十数合破阵防坚身当矢石 - He met the rebel army at the Jiang Bridge and fought for several rounds, breaking through their defenses and facing enemy arrows and stones.
  • 郡民老幼皆号泣曰杀我总督官我尚何生为 - The old and young of the city cried out, saying, 'They have killed our governor; how can we live on?'
  • 壮者助中军殊死战 - The strong men joined the main army in a desperate fight.
  • 台军一败涂地 - The rebel army was completely defeated.
  • 屠其二营 - They slaughtered the two camps.
  • 入拜家姬侍奴死者相枕藉 - They entered Bai's home and killed his concubines, servants, and slaves, who lay dead in piles.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 特穆尔 - Refers to Temujin, the Mongol leader who later became Genghis Khan. However, in this context, it likely refers to a different person with a similar name.
  • 方国珍 - Refers to Fang Guozhen, a prominent figure during the late Yuan and early Ming periods.
  • 曹娥江 - Refers to the Ca'e River, a river in Zhejiang Province.
  • 平章 - Refers to a high-ranking official in the Yuan dynasty.
  • 万戸 - Refers to a military rank in the Yuan dynasty.
  • 冯某 - Refers to an unnamed general under Fang Guozhen.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 一女为队官陈某所掠 - A woman was abducted by the captain T某某; '掠' means 'to seize or abduct.'
  • 举君尸无元大索三日 - Search for the body of the deceased for three days without success; '无元' implies 'without finding' or 'without result.'
  • 得于溺池中 - Found in a drowning pool, referring to a place where someone drowned.
  • 民搜见之齐唾其面且骂曰瞎贼 - People searched for him and when they found him, spat on his face and called him a blind thief; '瞎贼' is a derogatory term for a blind thief.
  • 我总督官何罪而令致于此耶 - Rhetorical question expressing indignation: 'What crime did our governor commit to be brought here like this?'
  • 不自杀执以归中冀中杀之 - Did not kill himself but was taken back to the camp where he was to be executed; '冀中' refers to the region or camp.
  • 中解其缚率诸军罗拜之 - Commander untied his bonds and led the troops to prostrate themselves before him; '罗拜' means 'to prostrate in a circle.'
  • 总督官忠肝义胆照映天地人神所共知 - Governor's loyalty and righteousness shine brightly in heaven and earth, known by both humans and gods; emphasizes high regard for the governor's character.
  • 公信任憸邪使国家之柱石陨于无辜 - Trusted the wicked, causing the pillars of the state to fall unjustly; '憸邪' refers to wicked or deceitful people.
  • 我之复讐明大义也 - Revenge is to uphold great righteousness; indicates a justification for revenge.
  • 杀我主将者既已斩之公幸毋罪 - Those who killed my commander have already been executed, please do not punish me; '毋罪' means 'do not punish.'
  • 我之罪尚何言尚何言 - What more can I say? Expresses deep regret or helplessness.
  • 继而军民为君持服为位以祭 - Subsequently, soldiers and civilians mourned for you and set up altars to offer sacrifices; '持服' means 'to wear mourning clothes.'
  • 私諡曰越民考越六日 - Privately posthumously honored you as the 'Exemplar of the People' for six days; '私諡' means 'private posthumous title.'
  • 一握兵柄志满意得酣觞废事轻谋首乱 - Once he took control of the military, became arrogant and indulged in drinking, neglecting duties and leading to chaos; '首乱' means 'to lead to disorder.'
  • 盖亦平昔越己之过有以酿成此祸与 - This was also due to past arrogance and mistakes, which led to this disaster; '酿成' means 'to brew or cause.'
  • 防中则老母稚子亦皆几上之肉耳 - Mother and children were also like meat on the chopping block; indicates their vulnerability.
  • 原其忠君爱民之心昞然与日星相昭明者 - Loyal heart to the emperor and love for the people are as bright as the sun and stars; emphasizes noble intentions.
  • 无可议也 - There is nothing to criticize; indicates pure intentions beyond reproach.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 队官陈某 - Refers to a military captain named T某某, indicating a specific individual in a historical context.

  • 圣人不得已而用之 - Sages use it only when absolutely necessary; indicates a cautious approach to warfare in Confucian thought.
  • 夫子必以临事而惧好谋而成荅子路行三军之问 - Refers to Confucius's teachings on the importance of being cautious and well-prepared in military matters, as recorded in the Analects.
  • 穆尔古蘓 - Refers to a historical figure, likely a military leader or official, whose actions are being discussed.

  • 轻谋首乱 - Refers to reckless planning and leading to chaos, indicating poor leadership and decision-making.
  • 几上之肉 - Metaphor for being at the mercy of others, indicating vulnerability and lack of protection.
  • 忠肝义胆 - Refers to loyalty and righteousness, indicating a noble character.
  • 照映天地人神所共知 - Emphasizes universal recognition of the governor's virtues, indicating widespread respect and admiration.
  • 国家之柱石 - Refers to the pillars of the state, indicating key figures who support the government.
  • 复讐明大义 - Refers to revenge carried out for the sake of justice, indicating a moral justification for revenge.
  • 杀我主将者既已斩之公幸毋罪 - Indicates a plea for leniency, suggesting that the speaker believes they have acted justly.
  • 持服 - Refers to wearing mourning clothes, indicating a formal expression of grief.
  • 为位以祭 - Refers to setting up altars for offerings, indicating a formal ritual of remembrance.
  • 私諡曰越民考越六日 - Indicates a private posthumous title given to the governor, suggesting a high level of respect and honor.
  • 一握兵柄志满意得酣觞废事轻谋首乱 - Describes the governor's downfall due to arrogance and poor leadership, indicating a cautionary tale.
  • 盖亦平昔越己之过有以酿成此祸与 - Suggests that the governor's past mistakes and arrogance led to his downfall, indicating a moral lesson.
  • 防中则老母稚子亦皆几上之肉耳 - Indicates the vulnerability of the governor's family, suggesting a tragic outcome.
  • 原其忠君爱民之心昞然与日星相昭明者 - Emphasizes the governor's noble intentions, indicating a positive aspect despite his downfall.
  • 无可议也 - Indicates that the governor's intentions were pure and beyond reproach, suggesting a moral judgment.

  • 拜与二子匿梵宇幽处民搜见之齐唾其面且骂曰瞎贼 - The captain hid with his two sons in a secluded place within a Buddhist temple, the people searched for him and when they found him, they spat on his face and called him a blind thief.
  • 我总督官何罪而令致于此耶不自杀执以归中冀中杀之中解其缚率诸军罗拜之 - What crime did our governor commit to be brought here like this? Did not kill himself but was taken back to the camp where he was to be executed, the commander untied his bonds and led the troops to prostrate themselves before him.
  • 总督官忠肝义胆照映天地人神所共知公信任憸邪使国家之柱石陨于无辜我之复讐明大义也 - Governor's loyalty and righteousness shine brightly in heaven and earth, known by both humans and gods; trusted the wicked, causing the pillars of the state to fall unjustly; revenge is to uphold great righteousness.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 拜珠为国大臣坐镇四省百官庶司孰不听令 - Rhetorical question emphasizing the authority of the official, suggesting that everyone obeys his commands.
  • 迈之不奉台檄擅兴师旅 - Someone who fails to follow orders; '台檄' refers to official documents or commands; '擅兴师旅' means initiating military campaigns without authorization.
  • 明问其罪黜之可也斩之可也 - Two options for dealing with the offender: either to demote him or to execute him.
  • 而乃阴结小丑作为此态 - '阴结小丑' means secretly aligning with petty villains; '作为此态' refers to acting in such a manner.
  • 是盗杀之非公论矣 - '盗杀之' means killing like a thief; '非公论矣' means it is not a fair judgment.
  • 民心之所以不服良以是也 - '民心之所以不服' means the reason why the people do not comply; '良以是也' means it is truly due to this.
  • 噫享有尊爵重禄而当国步艰难之日既不思防埃补报之道又不责自己贪饕之非反以谋害忠良为先务谓之无罪得乎 - Criticizes the official for not considering how to repay the state during difficult times and for blaming others instead of himself, suggesting that he should not be considered innocent.
  • 故其妻妾子女遭罹戮辱实自取之尚复可怜哉 - '故其妻妾子女遭罹戮辱' means therefore his wife, concubines, and children suffered punishment and humiliation; '实自取之' means it was indeed self-inflicted; '尚复可怜哉' means yet they are still pitiable.
  • 三姑六婆 - Traditional Chinese term referring to various types of women who were often seen as troublemakers.
  • 三姑者尼姑道姑卦姑也 - '三姑' refers to three types of women: nuns, Taoist nuns, and fortune-tellers.
  • 六婆者牙婆媒婆师婆防婆药婆稳婆也 - '六婆' refers to six types of women: matchmakers, midwives, fortune-tellers, nurses, herbalists, and wet nurses.
  • 盖与三刑六害同也 - Similar to the three punishments and six harms.
  • 人家有一于此而不致奸盗者几希矣 - If there is one of these in a household; '不致奸盗者几希矣' means it is rare for it not to lead to adultery or theft.
  • 能谨而逺之如避蛇蝎庶乎净宅之法 - To be cautious and keep away from them; '如避蛇蝎' means like avoiding snakes and scorpions; '庶乎净宅之法' means it is a method for keeping the home clean.
  • 不中用 - Means 'useless' or 'ineffective.'
  • 不可用也 - Means 'cannot be used.'
  • 克于先大夫无能为役 - To be able to serve one's ancestors; '无能为役' means unable to serve as a servant.
  • 不中为之役使 - Not suitable to serve as a servant.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 光致致唐尺短 - Indicates that the Tang dynasty's measurement standards were shorter when compared to modern times.
  • 以今校之亦自小也 - Comparative statement meaning 'when compared to today, they were also small.'
  • 不言其弓 - Indicates that the text does not mention the bow.
  • 道山新闻云 - Introduces a report or news item from the Dao Shan News.
  • 李后主宫嫔窅娘纎丽善舞 - Describes Empress Li's palace maiden Yǎo Niáng as slender, beautiful, and skilled in dance.
  • 后主作金莲髙六尺饰以寳物细带缨络莲中作品色瑞莲 - Describes the emperor creating a golden lotus six feet high, decorated with precious objects, fine ribbons, and a variety of auspicious lotuses inside.
  • 令窅娘以帛绕脚令纎小屈上作新月状素韈舞云中回旋有凌云之态 - Describes how Yǎo Niáng was instructed to wrap her feet with silk to make them slender and small, bend them into a crescent shape, and dance in white socks, spinning in the clouds with a soaring appearance.
  • 唐镐诗曰 - Indicates that Tang Hao wrote a poem.
  • 莲中花更好云里月长新 - A line from Tang Hao's poem, describing the beauty of the lotus and the moon.
  • 因窅娘作也 - Indicates that the poem was written because of Yǎo Niáng.
  • 由是人皆效之以纎弓为妙 - Means 'therefore, people all imitated her, considering slender feet to be the best.'
  • 以此知札脚自五代以来方为之如熈寜元丰以前人犹为者少近年则人人相效以不为者为耻也 - A conclusion indicating that foot binding began in the Five Dynasties period, was less common before the Xining and Yuanfeng eras, but became widespread and shameful not to do in recent times.
  • 溺水不跃 - Indicates that a woman did not jump out of the water.
  • 漳州龙溪县澳里人陈端才之妻蔡氏三玉 - Introduces the main character, Cai Sanyu, wife of Chen Duocai from Zhangzhou Longxi County.
  • 后至元间本处防起掠其里里媪集里中妇女同舟避难防追及 - Describes a situation where local authorities began to raid the village, and the women gathered on a boat to escape.
  • 三玉亟以水渍衣防视三玉有姿色欲先污之 - Describes how Cai Sanyu quickly wet her clothes to avoid being seen by the raiders, who were interested in her beauty.
  • 三玉绐曰衣湿更求衣间防取衣投水死防曰溺者必跃以长竿络钩俟其跃而举之尸竟不跃 - Describes how Cai Sanyu tricked the raiders by saying she needed to change her wet clothes, and when they took her clothes and threw them into the water, she did not jump out.
  • 防退三玉之父端广舟次上流尸遂逆流附父舟捭之不去移舟遡河而上尸从之上者三父异甚视则其女也 - Describes how the raiders retreated, and Cai Sanyu's father, Chen Duan Guang, moved the boat upstream, and the body of Cai Sanyu followed the boat three times, which was seen as a miraculous event.
  • 夫三玉一妇人耳宁死不辱出于天性宜其贞爽不昧如此 - Concludes that Cai Sanyu, as a woman, preferred to die rather than be dishonored, which was in line with her natural character and virtue.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 象曰 - Phrase used in the *I Ching* to introduce commentary on a hexagram or line, meaning 'The Image says' or 'The Hexagram says.'
  • 以辊受爵 - Unusual phrase, possibly a typo or mistranscription. Likely intended to be '以爵受爵' (to receive a title with a title), indicating a formality or ceremony, or it could refer to a specific ritual or object.
  • 亦不足敬也 - Means 'is also not worthy of respect.'
  • 此篇或者又谓自宋末即有非潘所造 - Suggests that there is a belief or claim that this text was not written by Pan, likely referring to a specific author named Pan.
  • 未审是否 - Means 'it is not known whether this is true' or 'it is unclear whether this is the case.'
  • 后平江蔡宗鲁【卫】作吝卦爻配之 - Indicates that after the time of the text, a person named Pianjiang Cai Zonglu (or possibly Cai Zonglu of Pingjiang) attempted to match or interpret the '吝卦' (Lìn guà, a hexagram) and its lines.
  • 曰吝亨利居闲不利有所为 - Direct quote from the hexagram or line, translating to 'It says: Lìn (stinginess), benefits from inaction, unfavorable for action.'
  • 利居闲无所求也不利有所为恐致祸也 - Explains the consequences of being stingy, stating that it is beneficial to remain inactive and not seek anything, but it is unfavorable to take action, as it may lead to disaster.
  • 初六居富吝于周急悔亡无攸利 - Describes the first line of the hexagram, meaning 'The first line: When wealthy, stingy in helping others in need, no regret, no benefit.'
  • 象曰吝于周急不恤其贫也 - Explains that the person is stingy in helping others in need, showing no concern for their poverty.
  • 悔亡无攸利己终有望也 - Means 'no regret, no benefit, but there is still hope in the end.'
  • 六二听妇言至吝不养其亲不恤其弟贞凶 - Describes the second line of the hexagram, meaning 'The second line: Listening to a woman's words, extremely stingy, not caring for one's parents, not caring for one's younger brothers, inauspicious.'
  • 象曰听妇言昵于私也不养其亲忘大恩也不恤其弟失大义也 - Explains that listening to a woman's words leads to favoring personal interests, neglecting one's parents (forgetting great kindness), and neglecting one's younger brothers (losing great righteousness).
  • 虽养弗时亦致灾也故贞凶 - Means 'Even if one tries to care for others, if it is not done at the right time, it will still bring disaster, so it is inauspicious.'
  • 九三极吝 - Describes the third line of the hexagram, meaning 'The third line: Extremely stingy.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 小人猶同也 - Literally 'small people are like this,' but likely refers to a group of people with similar characteristics, possibly negative.
  • 不利于君子 - Means 'not beneficial to the virtuous person.'
  • 入于邪也 - Means 'to enter into evil' or 'to become corrupted.'
  • 丽口掉舌 - Refers to using smooth or persuasive speech, often with a negative connotation.
  • 谝君子以求名干禄 - Means 'to flatter the virtuous to gain fame and profit.'
  • 同朋于谝 - Means 'to join in flattering' or 'to associate with those who flatter.'
  • 略施于民 - Means 'to slightly benefit the people.'
  • 新众听 - Means 'to refresh the people's ears' or 'to renew the people's attention.'
  • 来其谝酒食用享 - Means 'to bring their flattery, wine, food, and offerings.'
  • 取则也 - Means 'to take as an example' or 'to follow as a model.'
  • 以崇功也 - Means 'to honor achievements' or 'to glorify accomplishments.'
  • 饰言如簧 - Refers to speech as smooth as a musical instrument, often with a negative connotation.
  • 娱彼心 - Means 'to entertain his heart' or 'to please his mind.'
  • 用获南金 - Means 'to obtain southern gold' or 'to gain wealth.'
  • 终岁弗寜后有凶 - Means 'all year long, there is no peace, and later there will be misfortune.'
  • 莾谝不已 - Means 'to flatter without end' or 'to continue flattering without stopping.'
  • 四方欲杀之 - Means 'the four directions want to kill him' or 'everyone wants to kill him.'

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 小人犹同也，不利于君子入于邪也 - This sentence can be read as 'small people are like this, and it is not beneficial to the virtuous to enter into evil.'
  • 象曰丽口掉舌谝君子以求名干禄 - This sentence can be read as 'The image says: using smooth speech to flatter the virtuous to gain fame and profit.'
  • 初九谝于同朋无咎 - This sentence can be read as 'At the beginning, flattery among friends is without blame.'
  • 九二略施于民吉 - This sentence can be read as 'At nine two, slightly benefiting the people is auspicious.'
  • 六三来其谝酒食用享 - This sentence can be read as 'At six three, bringing their flattery, wine, food, and offerings.'
  • 九四饰言如簧以娱彼心用获南金 - This sentence can be read as 'At nine four, using speech as smooth as a musical instrument to entertain his heart and obtain southern gold.'
  • 九五君子终日高谝王用征安车以迎终岁弗寜后有凶 - This sentence can be read as 'At nine five, the virtuous person spends the whole day in high praise, and the king uses a safe carriage to welcome, but all year long, there is no peace, and later there will be misfortune.'
  • 上六莾谝不已四方欲杀之 - This sentence can be read as 'At upper six, excessive flattery without end leads to everyone wanting to kill him.'

  • 舶上人挈出之 - Means 'people on the ship pulling them out,' describing the process of retrieving the divers.
  • 于鼋鼍蛟龙之腹者比比有焉 - Means 'those who are buried in the bellies of turtles, crocodiles, and dragons are many,' referring to the dangers faced by the divers.
  • 有司名曰烏蜑戸 - Means 'the authorities named them as U-Dan households,' indicating the official recognition of these divers.
  • 特防故免 - Means 'especially exempted due to special circumstances,' suggesting a special exemption granted to these divers.
  • 江西行省叅知政事 - Refers to a high-ranking official in the Jiangxi provincial government.
  • 俾該管掾史立案 - Means 'ordering the relevant clerks to file a record,' indicating the administrative action taken.

  • 他省官曰中书咨文无是 - Means 'officials from other provinces said there was no such thing in the central government's documents,' indicating a lack of official recognition.

  • 文选曹公檄吴将校部曲文年月朔日子注发檄时也 - A title and annotation for a document, indicating it is a text selected from the *Wenxuan* by Cao Cao, a proclamation sent to the generals and troops of Wu, annotated and issued as a proclamation at that time.
  • 辍耕録卷十 - Title of a chapter in the *Chuogenglu* (Records of Interruptions in Ploughing), a historical record compiled by Ma Duanlin during the Yuan dynasty.
  • 钦定四库全书 - Indicates the work is part of the *Siku Quanshu* (Complete Library in Four Branches of Literature), a massive collection of Chinese literature commissioned by the Qing dynasty.
  • 王思善【绎】自号痴絶生 - Introduces Wang Sishan, a self-styled individual who calls himself 'Chijuesheng.' The use of '【绎】' suggests a commentary or explanation of his name.
  • 笃志好学雅有才思 - Describes Wang Sishan as someone deeply devoted to learning, with a refined and talented mind.

  • 檇李叶居仲【广居】寓思善之东里 - Indicates that Ye Juzhong lived in a place called 'East Village' near Wang Sishan.
  • 教授余从永嘉李五峯先生【孝光】往访之时 - Describes a visit made by the author to Ye Juzhong, accompanied by Li Wufeng, a respected teacher from Yongjia.
  • 已能丹青亦能写真 - Means Wang Sishan was already skilled in painting and could create portraits.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 凡写像须通晓相法 - Formal statement meaning 'In painting portraits, one must understand physiognomy.'
  • 闭目如在目前放笔如在笔底 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'to close one's eyes as if the subject is right before them, and to let the brush move as if it is at the tip of the hand.'
  • 淡墨覇定逐旋积起 - Means 'to use light ink to outline and gradually build up the details.'
  • 近代俗工胶柱鼓瑟不知变通之道 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'modern craftsmen who stick to a fixed method without adapting.'
  • 必欲其正襟危坐如泥塑人方乃传写 - Means 'they insist on having the subject sit stiffly like a statue before they can paint.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 相法 - Refers to the traditional Chinese practice of physiognomy, which involves interpreting a person's character and destiny based on their facial features.
  • 五岳四渎 - Traditional terms used in Chinese physiognomy to describe the five peaks and four rivers of the face, which are believed to correspond to different aspects of a person's character.
  • 四时气色 - Refers to the changes in a person's facial color due to the seasons, reflecting their health and emotional state.
  • 兰台廷尉 - Refers to the forehead and nose bridge, which are important areas in Chinese physiognomy.
  • 人中 - The philtrum, the groove between the nose and upper lip.
  • 眼堂 - The eye sockets.
  • 打圏 - Refers to the circular strokes used in painting the face, particularly around the eyes and mouth.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The opening statement '凡写像须通晓相法' sets the tone for the entire passage, emphasizing the importance of physiognomy in portraiture.
  • The sentence '人之面貎部位与夫五岳四渎各各不侔自有相对照处' describes the unique features of each person's face and how they correspond to traditional physiognomic terms.
  • The clause '四时气色亦异彼方呌啸谈话之间本真性情发见' links the changing appearance of a person to their true nature, which is revealed through their speech and actions.
  • The metaphorical expression '我则静而求之黙识于心闭目如在目前放笔如在笔底' describes the artist's state of mind and technique.
  • The sequence '淡墨覇定逐旋积起先兰台廷尉次鼻凖鼻凖既成以之为主' outlines the step-by-step process of painting a portrait, starting with the most prominent features.
  • The description '山根髙取印堂一笔下来或不髙不低在乎八九分中则侧边一笔下来' describes the flexible approach to painting the nose, depending on its position.
  • The list '次人中次口次眼堂次眼次眉次额次颊次髪际次耳次髪次头次打圏打圏者面部也' lists the order in which the rest of the face is painted, emphasizing the systematic approach.
  • The conclusion '必宜如此一一对去庶几无纖毫遗失' emphasizes the importance of following a systematic approach.
  • The criticism '近代俗工胶柱鼓瑟不知变通之道' criticizes modern artists who lack flexibility and adaptability.
  • The rigidity '必欲其正襟危坐如泥塑人方乃传写' criticizes the rigidity of some artists who insist on a fixed pose.
  • The frustration '因是万无一得此又何足怪哉吁吾不可柰何矣' concludes the passage with a sigh of frustration.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 采绘法 - Traditional Chinese painting technique, particularly for portrait painting.
  • 先用三朱腻粉方粉藤黄檀子土黄京墨合和衬底 - Complex mixture of pigments and powders used as a base layer in painting.
  • 仍用底粉薄笼 - Lightly apply a thin layer of powder over the base.
  • 斡染面色 - Technique of blending and shading facial colors.
  • 白者粉入少土黄 - Add a small amount of yellow earth to white powder for a specific shade.
  • 燕支不用胭脂 - Indicates that '燕支' (a type of red pigment) should not be used in place of '胭脂' (another type of red pigment).
  • 堂者粉檀子老青入少胭脂 - Add a small amount of '胭脂' to '粉檀子老青' (a mixture of powder, sandalwood, and blue) for a specific shade.
  • 黄者粉土黄入少土朱 - Add a small amount of '土朱' (a type of red pigment) to '粉土黄' (a mixture of powder and yellow earth) for a specific shade.
  • 青黒者粉入檀子土黄老青各一防粉薄罩檀墨斡已上看顔色清浊加减用 - Complex layering technique using various pigments and powders to achieve a specific shade of blue-black, with adjustments based on the final appearance.
  • 口角燕支淡如要带笑容口角两笔畧放起 - Lightly apply '燕支' to the corners of the mouth to create a subtle smile.
  • 眼中白染瞳子外两笔次用烟子防睛墨打圏眼梢防起有折便笑 - Technique of painting the whites of the eyes, the pupils, and the eyelids, with a final touch to create a subtle smile.
  • 口唇上胭脂蓦 - Apply '胭脂' to the lips.
  • 鼻色红胭脂防笼 - Apply '胭脂' to the nose.
  • 面雀斑淡墨水斡麻檀水斡 - Technique of painting freckles using diluted ink and '麻檀水' (a type of ink).
  • 髯色黒者依髪渲紫者檀墨间渲黄红者藤黄檀子渲 - Technique of painting different shades of hair and beard using various pigments.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 婦人則多以口腹为事 - '婦人則多' means 'women are mostly'; '以口腹为事' means 'preoccupied with food and drink.'

  • 惟尚新出而价贵者 - '惟尚' means 'only value'; '新出而价贵者' means 'new and expensive items.'
  • 稍贱便鄙之 - '稍贱' means 'slightly cheaper'; '便鄙之' means 'they consider them inferior.'
  • 至正己亥冬十二月 - '至正己亥' refers to the year 1359 in the Yuan dynasty; '冬十二月' means 'December.'
  • 金陵游军斩闗而入 - '金陵' is another name for Nanjing; '游军' means 'roving troops'; '斩闗而入' means 'cut through the gates and entered.'
  • 突至城下 - '突至' means 'suddenly arrived'; '城下' means 'at the city walls.'
  • 城门闭三月余 - '城门闭' means 'the city gates were closed'; '三月余' means 'for more than three months.'
  • 各路粮道不通 - '各路' means 'all routes'; '粮道' means 'supply lines'; '不通' means 'were blocked.'
  • 城中米价踊贵 - '城中' means 'in the city'; '米价踊贵' means 'the price of rice skyrocketed.'
  • 一斗直二十五缗 - '一斗' means 'one dou' (a unit of measure); '直' means 'worth'; '二十五缗' means 'twenty-five strings of cash.'
  • 越数日米既尽 - '越数日' means 'after several days'; '米既尽' means 'the rice was all gone.'
  • 糟糠亦与常日米价等 - '糟糠' refers to coarse food; '亦与常日米价等' means 'also became as expensive as rice on normal days.'
  • 有赀力人则得食 - '有赀力' means 'those with wealth'; '则得食' means 'were able to eat.'
  • 贫者不能也 - '贫者' means 'the poor'; '不能也' means 'could not.'
  • 又数日糟糠亦尽 - '又数日' means 'after several more days'; '糟糠亦尽' means 'the coarse food was also gone.'
  • 乃以油车家糠饼捣屑啖之 - '乃以' means 'then used'; '油车家' means 'oil mill'; '糠饼' means 'bran cakes'; '捣屑' means 'ground into powder'; '啖之' means 'ate them.'
  • 老幼妇女三五为羣行乞于市 - '老幼妇女' means 'elderly, young, and women'; '三五为羣' means 'in groups of three or five'; '行乞于市' means 'begged in the market.'
  • 虽姿色艶丽而衣裳济楚不暇自愧也 - '虽姿色艶丽' means 'even though they were beautiful'; '衣裳济楚' means 'their clothes were neat'; '不暇自愧也' means 'they had no time to feel ashamed.'
  • 至有合家父子夫妇兄弟结袂把臂共沈于水 - '至有' means 'there were even'; '合家' means 'the whole family'; '父子夫妇兄弟' means 'parents, children, spouses, and siblings'; '结袂把臂' means 'holding hands and arms'; '共沈于水' means 'drowned together in the water.'
  • 一城之人饿死者十六七 - '一城之人' means 'the people of the city'; '饿死者' means 'those who died of starvation'; '十六七' means 'sixteen or seventeen out of twenty.'
  • 军既退吴淞米航辐辏借以活而又太半病疫死 - '军既退' means 'after the army withdrew'; '吴淞米航' means 'rice ships from Wusong'; '辐辏' means 'converged'; '借以活' means 'to survive'; '而又太半病疫死' means 'and most of them died of illness.'

  • 至正丙申春今张太尉【士诚】未归顺时伪称诚王国号大周改元天佑歴日明时由淮渡浙攻破平江即承天以居尽撤去殿上像设坐于其中且以僧元凿字名其阁岂亦有定数乎 - In the spring of the Bing Shen year of the Zhizheng era, when Zhang Taibao (Shicheng) had not yet surrendered, he falsely claimed to be the king of Chengguo, established the state of Great Zhou, changed the era name to Tianyou, and when the Ming dynasty forces crossed from Huai to Zhejiang, they broke into Pingjiang and occupied Chengtian Temple, removing all images and sitting there, naming the pavilion after the monk Yuan who carved the words, was this also predestined?
  • 阿癐癐 - Ah Yiyi, a term used in military context.
  • 淮人防江南日于临阵之际齐声大喊阿癐癐以助军威 - When the Huai people were defending the south, they shouted 'Ah Yiyi' in unison during battle to boost morale.
  • 按朝野佥载武后时沧州南皮县丞郭胜静每廵乡唤百姓妇托以缝补而奸之其夫至缚胜静鞭数十主簿李懋往救解之胜静羞讳其事低身荅云忍痛不得口唱阿癐癐胜静不被打阿癐癐据此乃有所本 - According to *Chao Ye Qian Zai*, during the reign of Empress Wu, the county magistrate of Nanpi in Cangzhou, Guo Shengjing, would call upon women in the village under the pretext of mending clothes and then commit adultery with them. His wife bound him and beat him dozens of times. The county clerk Li Mao came to rescue him, and Guo Shengjing, ashamed and unwilling to admit his wrongdoing, replied, 'I cannot bear the pain, so I sing 'Ah Yiyi.' Guo Shengjing was not beaten because of 'Ah Yiyi.' This is the origin of the term.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 厕筹 - Literal term referring to wooden sticks used in ancient China for cleaning after using the toilet; no direct English equivalent.
  • 北史齐文宣王嗜酒淫泆肆行狂暴 - Describes Emperor Wenxuan of Northern Qi's excessive drinking and wild behavior; '嗜酒' means 'excessive drinking,' '淫泆' means 'wild and unrestrained,' and '肆行狂暴' means 'acting wildly and violently.'
  • 虽以杨愔为相使进厕筹 - Indicates that despite appointing Yang Yin as chancellor, Emperor Wenxuan still engaged in his wild behavior; '虽以' means 'even though he appointed,' '为相' means 'as chancellor,' and '使进厕筹' means 'to bring in the toilet sticks.'
  • 如淳曰厕音则谓牀边据牀视之一云溷厕也厕牀边侧 - Commentary by Ruchun on the meaning of '厕'; '如淳曰' means 'Ruchun says,' '厕音则' means 'the sound of '厕' is,' '谓牀边' means 'refers to the side of a bed,' '据牀视之' means 'sitting on the bed and looking at it,' and '一云溷厕也' means 'another interpretation is 'toilet and latrine.'
  • 汉书注如淳曰厕溷也孟康曰厕边侧也师古曰如说是也 - Commentary by Ruchun and Meng Kang on the meaning of '厕'; '汉书注' is a commentary on the *Book of Han*, '如淳曰' means 'Ruchun says,' '厕溷也' means 'toilet and latrine,' '孟康曰' means 'Meng Kang says,' '厕边侧也' means 'the side of the toilet,' and '师古曰如说是也' means 'the scholar says that Ruchun's interpretation is correct.'
  • 汉文居防北临厕使慎夫人鼓瑟 - Event in the *Book of Han* where Emperor Wen sat on the north side of the toilet and had his favorite concubine, Shen Fu Ren, play the zither; '汉文' is Emperor Wen, '居防北临厕' means 'sat on the north side of the toilet,' and '使慎夫人鼓瑟' means 'had Shen Fu Ren play the zither.'

  • 北史 - The *Northern Dynasties History*, a historical record of the Northern Dynasties period.
  • 齐文宣王 - Emperor Wenxuan of Northern Qi, a ruler known for his excessive drinking and wild behavior.
  • 杨愔 - Yang Yin, a chancellor during the Northern Qi dynasty.
  • 史记 - The *Records of the Grand Historian*, a historical text compiled by Sima Qian.
  • 汉书 - The *Book of Han*, a historical text compiled by Ban Gu.
  • 汉文 - Emperor Wen of Han, a ruler known for his frugality and benevolence.
  • 慎夫人 - Shen Fu Ren, a favorite concubine of Emperor Wen.
  • 韦昭 - Wei Zhao, a scholar and commentator on the *Book of Han*.
  • 郅都 - Grand Marshal Zhi Du, a historical figure known for his strict governance.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 隂陽炭烧空欲海修持行满 - Combination of yin and yang, charcoal burning, emptiness, ocean of desire, practice, and fulfillment of actions. Suggests a spiritual journey or practice aimed at achieving emptiness and fulfilling one's actions.
  • 雌雄剑劈破愁城 - Male and female swords and cutting through the city of sorrow. Metaphor for overcoming deep sorrow or distress using a powerful tool or method.
  • 七星冠刚替下鳯头钗 - Seven-star crown and phoenix-headed hairpin. Describes a change in appearance or status, possibly symbolizing a transformation or a shift in identity.
  • 合欢带生纫做鹿皮袋 - Joyful belt and deer skin bag. Suggests a transformation from a decorative item to a practical one, possibly symbolizing a change from luxury to simplicity.
  • 空非空色非色色即是空 - Reference to Buddhist philosophy, suggesting that form is ultimately empty and that emptiness is the true nature of reality.
  • 道可道名可名强名曰道 - From Buddhist or Taoist philosophy, suggesting that the true nature of the Dao is beyond words and names, but it is called the Dao to facilitate understanding.

  • 欲因动之 - 欲 (wanted), 因 (to take advantage of), 动 (to move), and 之 (her). This phrase indicates that someone wanted to take advantage of the situation to move the wife.
  • 大人以爱子许人必慎所择矣 - 大人 (parents), 以 (with), 爱子 (loving son), 许 (agree), 人 (to someone), 必 (must), 慎 (be cautious), 所择 (the choice), and 矣 (a particle indicating a completed action). This phrase indicates that the parents, having agreed to give their loving son to someone, must be cautious in their choice.
  • 即有不讳命也 - 即 (if), 有 (there is), 不讳 (unavoidable), 命 (fate), and 也 (a particle indicating a completed action). This phrase indicates that if there is an unavoidable fate, it is so.
  • 若等谓我且慕世俗富贵而改聘耶 - 若等 (you all), 谓 (say), 我 (I), 且 (and), 慕 (admire), 世俗 (worldly), 富贵 (wealth and honor), 而 (but), 改聘 (change the engagement), and 耶 (a particle indicating a question). This phrase indicates that the family was saying that the wife would admire worldly wealth and honor and change the engagement.
  • 有死而已 - 有 (only), 死 (die), and 而已 (that's all). This phrase indicates that the wife would rather die than change the engagement.
  • 皆愧谢不敢复言 - 皆 (all), 愧 (feel ashamed), 谢 (apologize), 不敢 (dare not), and 复言 (say again). This phrase indicates that everyone felt ashamed and apologized, daring not to speak again.
  • 事继母尽孝道 - 事 (serve), 继母 (stepmother), 尽 (fully), and 孝道 (filial piety). This phrase indicates that the wife served her stepmother with full filial piety.
  • 死之日母大恸 - 死之日 (on the day of her death), 母 (mother), 大 (greatly), and 恸 (grieve). This phrase indicates that the mother greatly grieved on the day of the wife's death.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 所偏爱即死必伤其心然终必死矣 - Complex and elliptical phrase meaning 'If one is overly fond of something, then even if it causes pain to the heart, in the end it will still lead to death.'
  • 为将奈何 - Rhetorical question meaning 'What is to be done?' or 'What can be done?' Expresses helplessness or uncertainty.
  • 君后富贵幸念之 - Polite and respectful request, likely from a subordinate to a superior, meaning 'If you later attain wealth and status, please remember [this].'
  • 言既复瞑目 - 'After he spoke, he closed his eyes.' May indicate the end of a person's life or a moment of finality.
  • 寓殡湘中 - 'He was temporarily buried in Xiangzhong.' '寓殡' means to temporarily inter a body before a proper burial.
  • 及序其遗藁云 - 'And he composed a preface for his collected works.' '遗藁' refers to a person's posthumous collected writings or manuscripts.
  • 非茍学所能至者 - 'Not something that can be achieved through hasty or superficial learning.' '茍学' means 'superficial learning' or 'learning done carelessly.'
  • 女子当治织絍组紃以致其孝敬 - 'A woman should manage weaving and sewing in order to express her filial piety and respect.' Reflects traditional Confucian ideals of women's roles in domestic and moral life.
  • 辞翰非所事也 - 'Writing and literary composition are not her concern.' '辞翰' refers to literary composition or writing.
  • 因出其藁得五言七首七言十一首 - 'Because of this, they retrieved her manuscripts and found five-character poems seven in number, seven-character poems eleven in number.' A precise enumeration of her poetic output.
  • 特为编集成帙题曰緑窻遗藁 - 'They specially compiled them into a volume and titled it *Lüchuang Yigao*.' '緑窻' is likely a poetic or symbolic title, possibly referring to a window or a place of quiet reflection.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 泰定五年八月廿有一日也 - Refers to the date 'the 21st day of the eighth month in the fifth year of the Taiping era' (Taiping being a reign title of Emperor Taiding of the Yuan dynasty). This is a precise historical date and must be retained in translation.
  • 后三日寓殡湘中 - 'Three days later, he was temporarily buried in Xiangzhong.' 'Xiangzhong' likely refers to a region in Hunan province, possibly near the Xiang River.
  • 故妻孙氏蕙兰 - 'His former wife, Sun Hui Lan.' This is a proper name and should be translated directly as a name.
  • 周卿先生以孝经论语及凡女诫之书敎之 - 'Mr. Zhou Qing, the gentleman, taught her using *The Classic of Filial Piety*, *Analects of Confucius*, and other books on female conduct.' Reflects the traditional Confucian education of women, emphasizing moral instruction and domestic virtue.
  • 因其弟受唐诗家法于庭取而读之 - 'Because her younger brother received instruction in the principles of Tang poetry at home, she took it up and read it.' Refers to the transmission of poetic technique within families, a common practice in classical Chinese literary culture.
  • 緑窻遗藁 - The title of the collected poems, likely symbolic. '緑窻' may refer to a green window, a common poetic image symbolizing quiet reflection or a place of literary creation.
  • 五言诗曰窻里人初起窻前 - This begins a five-character poem. The phrase '窻里人初起窻前' may be the start of a poem about a person rising at the window, or the window itself as a subject.

Proper Nouns

  • 泰定五年 - 'The fifth year of the Taiping era' (Taiping being the reign title of Emperor Taiding of the Yuan dynasty). This is a historical date and should be translated as such.
  • 湘中 - A region in Hunan province, likely near the Xiang River. This is a geographical reference and should be translated as 'Xiangzhong' or 'central Xiang region.'
  • 孙氏蕙兰 - 'Sun Hui Lan,' the name of the woman. This is a proper name and should be translated directly.
  • 周卿先生 - 'Mr. Zhou Qing,' the name of the teacher. This is a proper name and should be translated directly.
  • 緑窻遗藁 - The title of the collected poems. '緑窻' is likely a poetic or symbolic title and should be translated as *Lüchuang Yigao* (Green Window Collected Manuscripts).
  • 五言诗曰窻里人初起窻前 - This is the beginning of a five-character poem. The title or beginning of the poem should be translated as is, unless it is a known poem.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 卷帘冲落絮 - Literal meaning: rolling up the curtain, the falling catkins rush out. Vivid image of springtime, where catkins (cotton-like fluff from trees) are blown by the wind.
  • 开镜见垂条 - Literal meaning: opening the mirror, seeing the drooping branches. Suggests a moment of reflection, both literal and metaphorical.
  • 坐对分金线 - Literal meaning: sitting opposite, dividing the golden thread. Could refer to embroidery or sewing, symbolizing delicate work or preparation.
  • 行防拂翠翘 - Literal meaning: walking and brushing the green feather. Likely refers to a woman brushing her hair or arranging her headdress, with '翠翘' (green feather) being a decorative hair ornament.
  • 流莺空巧语 - Literal meaning: flowing oriole, empty clever words. Suggests the sound of birdsong, which is often used in poetry to evoke a sense of beauty or melancholy.
  • 倦听不须调 - Literal meaning: tired of listening, no need to adjust. Implies a state of weariness or indifference, possibly toward the sounds of nature or someone else's speech.
  • 小阁烹香茗 - Literal meaning: in the small pavilion, brewing fragrant tea. Sets the scene of a quiet, refined space.
  • 防帘下玉钩 - Literal meaning: under the curtain, the jade hook. Likely refers to a decorative curtain or window treatment, with '玉钩' (jade hook) being a luxurious detail.
  • 灯光翻出鼎 - Literal meaning: the light flickers out of the tripod. Could symbolize the warmth and comfort of a home, with the tripod (鼎) being an ancient cooking vessel.
  • 钗影倒沉瓯 - Literal meaning: the hairpin's shadow casts down into the cup. Suggests a moment of stillness, where the reflection of a hairpin is seen in a cup of tea.
  • 婢捧消春困 - Literal meaning: the maid holds something to dispel spring fatigue. Implies that the maid is bringing refreshment or comfort to the person resting.
  • 亲尝散暮愁 - Literal meaning: tasting personally to disperse evening sorrow. Suggests a moment of solace, where the act of tasting (perhaps tea or food) brings relief from sadness.
  • 吟诗因坐久 - Literal meaning: reciting poetry because of sitting for a long time. Implies that the person has been sitting for so long that they have turned to poetry as a form of entertainment or reflection.
  • 灯前催晓妆 - Literal meaning: before the lamp, urging morning makeup. Implies that the person is preparing for the day, possibly in the early morning hours.
  • 但愿梅花月 - Literal meaning: only wishing for the plum blossom moon. Poetic expression, likely referring to a beautiful or auspicious moon, with '梅花' (plum blossom) symbolizing purity or resilience.
  • 采阁闭朝寒 - Literal meaning: the gathering pavilion closed in the morning cold. Suggests a cold, early morning, possibly with the person inside a pavilion.
  • 妆成拟问安 - Literal meaning: makeup completed, ready to inquire about peace. Implies that the person has finished preparing and is ready to greet others or perform a ritual.
  • 忽闻春雪下 - Literal meaning: suddenly hear the spring snow falling. Suggests a sudden change in weather, possibly bringing a sense of surprise or wonder.
  • 唤婢卷帘看 - Literal meaning: call the maid to roll up the curtain and look. Implies that the person is curious about the outside world and wants to see the snow.
  • 粲粲梅花 - Literal meaning: bright and shining, the plum blossom blooms. Suggests a beautiful, vibrant plum blossom.
  • 盈盈似玉人 - Literal meaning: graceful and full, like a jade person. Suggests that the plum blossom is both beautiful and delicate, like a person made of jade.
  • 甘心对氷雪 - Literal meaning: willing to face ice and snow. Suggests a willingness to endure harsh conditions, possibly as a metaphor for resilience.
  • 不爱艶阳春 - Literal meaning: do not love the bright spring. Suggests a preference for something more understated or challenging, rather than the usual beauty of spring.
  • 小小春罗扇 - Literal meaning: small spring silk fan. Suggests a delicate, elegant object, possibly used for cooling oneself.
  • 团团秋月生 - Literal meaning: round and round, the autumn moon rises. Suggests the beauty of the full moon, possibly symbolizing completeness or harmony.
  • 蟠桃花里 - Literal meaning: in the blooming peach blossom. Suggests a beautiful, vibrant scene, possibly with peach blossoms in full bloom.
  • 绣得董防成 - Literal meaning: embroidered, the shield of Dǒng Fáng is complete. Likely refers to a famous shield from Chinese history, possibly symbolizing protection or strength.
  • 自拂防眉黛 - Literal meaning: gently brush the eyebrows with black ink. Suggests a moment of self-care or preparation, possibly in the morning.
  • 何曽惯得愁 - Literal meaning: never accustomed to sorrow. Suggests that the person is not used to feeling sad or troubled.
  • 若教如翠栁 - Literal meaning: if taught to be like the green willow. Suggests a comparison to a willow tree, which is often associated with grace or flexibility.
  • 便恐不禁秋 - Literal meaning: then fear not to withstand the autumn. Suggests that the person fears they would not be able to endure the hardships of autumn, possibly metaphorically.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 梅花 - A symbol of resilience, purity, and endurance in Chinese culture, often associated with winter and the arrival of spring.
  • 玉人 - A term often used to describe a beautiful or graceful person, often with connotations of elegance and refinement.
  • 董防 - A reference to a famous shield from Chinese history, possibly symbolizing protection or strength.
  • 翠栁 - A symbol of grace, flexibility, and resilience, often associated with spring and the arrival of new life.
  • 七言诗 - A traditional form of Chinese poetry, often used to express deep emotions or philosophical reflections.

  • 緑窻寂寞掩残春 - Literally 'the green window lonely, closing the remaining spring,' it conveys a sense of solitude and the end of spring.

  • 可怜娇怯性偏灵 - Describes 'pitifully, her tender and timid nature is especially sensitive.'

  • 几防梅花发小盆 - Suggests 'a few pots of plum blossoms are about to bloom,' indicating the approach of winter or early spring.
  • 氷肌玉骨伴黄昏 - Uses a metaphor to describe 'with icy skin and jade bones, she accompanies the twilight.'
  • 闲划金钗记月防 - Means 'leisurely, she uses a golden hairpin to mark the moon's phases,' a poetic way of keeping track of time.
  • 绣被寒多未欲眠 - Indicates 'the embroidered quilt is cold, and she does not want to sleep,' suggesting discomfort due to the cold.
  • 梨花枝上听春鹃 - Means 'she listens to the cuckoo on the pear blossom branch,' where the cuckoo is a symbol of spring and sometimes associated with melancholy.
  • 明朝又是清明节 - Refers to 'tomorrow is the Qingming Festival,' a traditional Chinese holiday for honoring ancestors.
  • 愁见人家买纸钱 - Expresses 'she worries about seeing others buy paper money,' where paper money is burned as an offering during the festival.
  • 春雨随风湿粉墙 - Describes 'spring rain brings moisture to the whitewashed walls,' indicating the dampness caused by the rain.

  • 流过长沟水亦香 - Suggests 'the water flowing through the long ditch is fragrant,' indicating the pleasant scent carried by the water.
  • 春风昨夜碧桃开 - Describes 'the spring wind last night opened the green peach blossoms,' indicating the blooming of peach flowers.
  • 正想瑶池月满台 - Imagines 'the moonlit platform in the Jade Pool,' where 'Jade Pool' (瑶池) is a mythical place in Chinese mythology.
  • 欲折一枝寄王母 - Means 'wanting to pick a branch to send to the Queen Mother,' where the Queen Mother (王母) is a figure in Chinese mythology.
  • 青鸾飞去几时来 - Refers to 'the blue phoenix flies away, when will it return?' where the blue phoenix (青鸾) is a mythical bird.
  • 小婢相随倚画防 - Indicates 'a young maid follows and leans against the painted screen,' suggesting a quiet, reflective moment.
  • 金钗误挂绯桃落 - Suggests 'a golden hairpin accidentally hangs on a red peach blossom,' indicating a moment of carelessness.
  • 罗袖愁依翠竹 - Uses a metaphor to describe 'the silk sleeve worries as it leans on the green bamboo,' indicating a state of melancholy.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 林空帷奠巾栉中房虚织絍辞章余婉娈琴瑟有余音睠言瞻故物恻怆内不任岂无新人好焉知谐我心掩穴抚长暮涕下霑衣襟 - Highly poetic line describing a scene of mourning and reflection, with the speaker looking at old objects and feeling deep sorrow.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 林空帷奠巾栉中房虚织絍辞章余婉娈琴瑟有余音睠言瞻故物恻怆内不任岂无新人好焉知谐我心掩穴抚长暮涕下霑衣襟 - Imagery of the empty room, old objects, and the sound of a lute (琴瑟) evokes a sense of mourning and nostalgia.
  • 人生贵有别室家各有宜贫贱逺结婚中心两不移前日良宴防今为死别离亲戚各在前临诀不成辞傍人拭我泪令我要裁悲共尽固人理谁能心勿思 - Reference to marriage and death is a common theme in classical Chinese literature, often used to reflect on the impermanence of life.
  • 感独曰幽幽蕙草晚靡靡兰芳断皎皎夜泉人防防不复旦流尘栖暗壁凉吹经虚幔无论懽意消日复愁思乱防伤夕方永气变秋将晏当窻惨断素捐箧悲柔翰忆初成好合誓且同忧患何言遂长终独处増永叹寤寝忽如在展转惊复散念兹何嗟及哀至聊自判百日曰人生 - Imagery of the evening, the changing seasons, and the passage of time reflects the classical Chinese theme of the transience of life.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 林空帷奠巾栉中房虚织絍辞章余婉娈琴瑟有余音睠言瞻故物恻怆内不任岂无新人好焉知谐我心掩穴抚长暮涕下霑衣襟 - Highly poetic and uses many classical allusions and metaphors, making it challenging to translate directly.
  • 人生贵有别室家各有宜贫贱逺结婚中心两不移前日良宴防今为死别离亲戚各在前临诀不成辞傍人拭我泪令我要裁悲共尽固人理谁能心勿思 - Discusses the nature of human relationships and the inevitability of change, particularly in the context of marriage and death.
  • 感独曰幽幽蕙草晚靡靡兰芳断皎皎夜泉人防防不复旦流尘栖暗壁凉吹经虚幔无论懽意消日复愁思乱防伤夕方永气变秋将晏当窻惨断素捐箧悲柔翰忆初成好合誓且同忧患何言遂长终独处増永叹寤寝忽如在展转惊复散念兹何嗟及哀至聊自判百日曰人生 - Reflects on the passage of time, the fading of joy, and the inevitability of sorrow and death.

[280]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 悲死別矧在心相知 - Compound expression meaning 'grief at death, especially when it is someone close to the heart.'
  • 新婚未及久 - Literally 'newly married not yet long,' indicating a short marriage.
  • 杳杳遽何之 - Poetic expression meaning 'suddenly and mysteriously gone where?'
  • 昔为连理木今为断肠枝 - Metaphor comparing past unity to current sorrow, using the imagery of twin trees (连理木) and broken branches (断肠枝).
  • 相去时几何百日奄在兹 - Temporal expression meaning 'how much time has passed, just a hundred days, yet it seems like an eternity.'
  • 月有圆夕逝水无还期 - Metaphor comparing the cyclical nature of the moon to the irreversible flow of time.
  • 弃置非人情何以为我思 - Rhetorical question meaning 'to abandon is not human nature, how can one not think of me?'
  • 入室曰妆阁闭长夜幽兰坐 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'entering the room, the dressing chamber is closed, sitting through the long night in seclusion.'
  • 复春犹疑挑锦字不见掩罗巾 - Poetic expression meaning 'still in spring, still doubtful, the embroidered letters are not seen, the silk handkerchief remains hidden.'
  • 故物空在目萧条生网尘 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'old objects remain visible, but they are now covered in dust, symbolizing decay and loss.'
  • 虚窻明月满芳砌緑苔滋 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'the empty window is filled with bright moonlight, the fragrant steps are covered with green moss.'
  • 花间时染翰尚忆解题诗 - Poetic expression meaning 'occasionally writing in the flowers, still remembering the poems that explained the themes.'
  • 寂寞幽泉下贞心空自知 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'in the solitude of the deep spring, the steadfast heart knows itself alone.'
  • 嗟夫孙氏之诗依乎礼义 - Concluding expression meaning 'alas, the poetry of the Sun family adheres to propriety and righteousness.'
  • 先生之诗哀而不伤举得性情之正是可传也已 - Concluding expression meaning 'the master's poetry is sorrowful but not excessive, capturing the true nature and emotions, and thus it is worthy of being passed down.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 孙氏 - Refers to the Sun family, likely a reference to a specific historical or literary family known for their poetry.
  • 先生 - Refers to a respected master or teacher, often used in classical Chinese to denote a person of high moral and intellectual standing.
  • 礼义 - Refers to propriety and righteousness, core values in Confucian philosophy.
  • 哀而不伤 - Confucian ideal of expressing sorrow without excess, maintaining a balanced emotional state.
  • 性情之正 - Refers to the true nature and emotions, emphasizing the authenticity and integrity of the poet's feelings.

Proper Nouns

  • 孙氏 - The Sun family, likely a reference to a specific historical or literary family.
  • 先生 - A respected master or teacher, often used in classical Chinese to denote a person of high moral and intellectual standing.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 為將嗜殺 - A phrase meaning 'a general who enjoys killing,' used to describe a military leader with a propensity for violence.
  • 盛暑中 - Refers to a period of intense heat, typically summer.
  • 憩道傍大下 - Means 'resting under a large tree by the roadside,' where  (likely a variant of '槐') refers to a type of tree.
  • 遽問曰 - A formal expression meaning 'asked hastily' or 'asked abruptly.'
  • 某是已 - A humble response meaning 'I am indeed' or 'I am the one.'
  • 隂府攝汝 - A phrase from the afterlife context, meaning 'the underworld has summoned you.'
  • 容到家與妻子一别可乎 - A request meaning 'may I go home to say goodbye to my wife and children?'
  • 比妻子來前 - Means 'by the time my wife and children arrived.'
  • 反接 - A term from legal or punitive contexts, meaning 'bound with hands behind the back' or 'restrained.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 王皮 - The name of the protagonist, a general or military leader.
  • 鳯翔府 - Refers to Fengxiang Prefecture, a historical administrative region in Shaanxi Province during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 秦白起 - Refers to Bai Qi, a famous general of the State of Qin during the Warring States period, known for his ruthless military campaigns.
  • 坑趙降卒四十萬 - Refers to the Massacre of Zhao at Changping, where Bai Qi allegedly killed 400,000 Zhao soldiers.
  • 隂府 - The underworld or realm of the dead in Chinese mythology.
  • 儀衛吏 - Refers to the ceremonial guards, officials, and servants in the underworld's court.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The opening phrase '為將嗜殺' sets the tone for the narrative but is elliptical, requiring context to fully understand the general's character.
  • The clause '隂府攝汝，王曰某平生無他過惡望賜矜怜' presents a dialogue between the general and the underworld officials, with the general pleading for mercy.
  • The general's request for a final goodbye, '容到家與妻子一别可乎,' is a poignant moment that requires careful rendering to convey its emotional weight.
  • The final phrase '乃反接' describes the general's punishment, but its meaning is somewhat ambiguous without context.

Proper Nouns

  • 王皮 - The name of the protagonist.
  • 鳯翔府 - Fengxiang Prefecture.
  • 秦白起 - Bai Qi, the general of the State of Qin.
  • 趙降卒 - Zhao soldiers who surrendered.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 火沟中而归 - Literal expression meaning 'returned by fire,' possibly referring to a return via a dangerous or unexpected route.
  • 司无以为养生计 - Means 'Mr. Si had no means to sustain himself.'
  • 即所偿钱为豆乳酿酒货卖以给食 - Means 'he used the money he had to repay to make soy milk, brew alcohol, and sell it to provide food.'
  • 李日益贫 - Means 'Liu became increasingly poor.'
  • 更十年李复出所佃田质陈氏 - Means 'ten years later, Liu again mortgaged the land he leased to Chen.'
  • 司记为李所辱时今幸可一报复遂具鸡酒饮亦如之 - Means 'Mr. Si remembered being humiliated by Liu and now had the opportunity to retaliate. He prepared chicken and wine to drink, just as Liu had done.'
  • 李忘前过不自责反怨薄已怒甚归积膏火破盎中夜抵司家 - Means 'Liu forgot his past wrongdoings, did not blame himself, but instead resented Mr. Si and became very angry. He returned home, collected oil, and broke a jar, coming to Mr. Si's house at night.'
  • 司妻方就蓐 - Means 'Mr. Si's wife was just giving birth.'
  • 李犹豫间闻人启户惧事觉遗火亟走 - Means 'Liu hesitated, heard someone opening the door, feared being discovered, and quickly fled, leaving the fire.'
  • 司家实不有人 - Means 'Mr. Si's house was actually empty.'
  • 旦得火器塲中验器底有李字 - Means 'the next day, they found the fire-setting device in the field and saw that the bottom of the device had Liu's name.'
  • 因悟昔我焚彼家以其家人产子不欲焚今彼焚我家而我之妻亦产子而不被焚此天也非人也 - Means 'they realized that when Mr. Si burned Liu's house, it was because Liu's family had a child, and he did not want to burn them. Now Liu burned Mr. Si's house, but Mr. Si's wife also had a child and was not burned. This must be fate, not a person.'
  • 持钱五千往 - Means 'he took five thousand coins to go.'
  • 昨日小人无状失礼义不得共饮兹愿少伸谢 - Means 'yesterday, I acted improperly and lost my manners, so we could not drink together. Now I wish to offer a small token of thanks.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 火沟中而归 - Refers to a dangerous or unexpected return, possibly alluding to a perilous journey.
  • 司无以为养生计 - Indicates a state of poverty and lack of resources.
  • 即所偿钱为豆乳酿酒货卖以给食 - Reflects a common practice in ancient China of using borrowed money to engage in small-scale business to sustain oneself.
  • 李日益贫 - Highlights the cyclical nature of poverty and wealth.
  • 更十年李复出所佃田质陈氏 - Indicates the practice of mortgaging land to borrow money, a common financial practice in ancient China.
  • 司记为李所辱时今幸可一报复遂具鸡酒饮亦如之 - Shows the human tendency to seek revenge, even in small matters.
  • 李忘前过不自责反怨薄已怒甚归积膏火破盎中夜抵司家 - Illustrates the cycle of resentment and anger.
  • 司妻方就蓐 - Refers to the birthing process, a significant event in ancient Chinese culture.
  • 李犹豫间闻人启户惧事觉遗火亟走 - Reflects the fear of discovery and the consequences of one's actions.
  • 司家实不有人 - Indicates a moment of relief and realization.
  • 旦得火器塲中验器底有李字 - Shows the importance of evidence in resolving disputes.
  • 因悟昔我焚彼家以其家人产子不欲焚今彼焚我家而我之妻亦产子而不被焚此天也非人也 - Reflects the belief in fate and divine intervention.
  • 持钱五千往 - Indicates a gesture of reconciliation and goodwill.
  • 昨日小人无状失礼义不得共饮兹愿少伸谢 - Shows the importance of social harmony and the desire to make amends.

Proper Nouns

  • 司 - Refers to Mr. Si, a person in the story.
  • 李 - Refers to Liu, another person in the story.
  • 陈氏 - Refers to Chen, a person or family.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 意幸毋督过 - Polite expression meaning 'I hope you will not be too strict' or 'I hope you will not criticize too harshly.'
  • 李疑绐以疾卧不起 - '李疑' is a proper name, '绐' means 'to deceive,' and '疾卧不起' means 'lying ill and not getting up.' Together, it means 'Li Yi deceived by claiming to be ill and not getting up.'
  • 强请不已 - Means 'persistently invited' or 'kept on inviting.'
  • 遂同之酒家邀酤儿与饮酒 - '遂' means 'then' or 'thus,' '同之' means 'together with him,' '酒家' means 'wine shop,' '邀' means 'invite,' '酤儿' means 'wine seller,' and '与饮酒' means 'to drink together.' Together, it means 'then they went to a wine shop together and invited the wine seller to drink.'
  • 半自起酌酒劝李 - '半' means 'halfway,' '自起' means 'got up himself,' '酌酒' means 'poured wine,' and '劝李' means 'to encourage Li.' Together, it means 'halfway, he got up himself, poured wine, and encouraged Li.'
  • 子之孙某年月日夜子时生 - '子之孙' means 'your grandson,' '某年月日夜子时' means 'on a certain day and time,' and '生' means 'born.' Together, it means 'your grandson was born on a certain day and time.'
  • 而吾子亦夜者子时生 - '而' means 'and,' '吾子' means 'my son,' '亦' means 'also,' '夜者子时' means 'at the same time at night,' and '生' means 'born.' Together, it means 'and my son was also born at the same time at night.'
  • 怨仇之事慎勿复为 - '怨仇之事' means 'matters of resentment and hatred,' '慎勿' means 'carefully do not,' and '复为' means 'repeat.' Together, it means 'carefully do not repeat matters of resentment and hatred.'
  • 具白前所仇事 - '具白' means 'fully explain,' '前' means 'previous,' '所仇事' means 'matters of resentment.' Together, it means 'fully explain the previous matters of resentment.'
  • 沥酒为誓 - '沥酒' means 'pouring wine,' and '为誓' means 'to take an oath.' Together, it means 'to take an oath by pouring wine.'
  • 语酤儿曰 - '语' means 'to say,' '酤儿' means 'wine seller,' and '曰' means 'said.' Together, it means 'he said to the wine seller.'
  • 子识之 - '子' means 'you,' '识' means 'remember,' and '之' means 'it.' Together, it means 'you remember it.'
  • 试用此警世间人 - '试' means 'try,' '用' means 'to use,' '此' means 'this,' '警' means 'warn,' and '世间人' means 'people of the world.' Together, it means 'try to use this to warn people of the world.'
  • 不善慎勿为也 - '不善' means 'bad,' '慎勿' means 'carefully do not,' and '为' means 'do.' Together, it means 'carefully do not do bad things.'
  • 剧饮尽欢 - '剧饮' means 'drank heavily,' and '尽欢' means 'to enjoy fully.' Together, it means 'drank heavily and enjoyed fully.'
  • 乃更约为婚姻 - '乃' means 'then,' '更约' means 'renewed the agreement,' and '为婚姻' means 'to become in-laws.' Together, it means 'then they renewed their agreement to become in-laws.'
  • 自是李亦不贫 - '自是' means 'from then on,' '李' means 'Li,' '亦' means 'also,' and '不贫' means 'not poor.' Together, it means 'from then on, Li was also not poor.'
  • 两家至今丰给 - '两家' means 'the two families,' '至今' means 'until now,' '丰给' means 'abundant and well-provided.' Together, it means 'the two families are still abundant and well-provided.'
  • 此在至正初元间 - '此' means 'this,' '在' means 'in,' '至正初元间' means 'the early years of the Zhi Zheng era.' Together, it means 'this happened in the early years of the Zhi Zheng era.'
  • 时天固已监之 - '时' means 'at that time,' '天' means 'heaven,' '固已' means 'already,' and '监之' means 'supervised it.' Together, it means 'at that time, heaven had already supervised it.'
  • 所以李不复可加害也 - '所以' means 'therefore,' '李' means 'Li,' '不复' means 'no longer,' '可加害' means 'could be harmed.' Together, it means 'therefore, Li could no longer be harmed.'
  • 向使司氏决快所欲 - '向使' means 'if only,' '司氏' means 'the wife of Si,' '决快所欲' means 'decided to satisfy her desires.' Together, it means 'if only the wife of Si decided to satisfy her desires.'
  • 未必能复田 - '未必' means 'may not,' '能' means 'can,' '复田' means 'return to farming.' Together, it means 'may not be able to return to farming.'
  • 纵复田 - '纵' means 'even if,' '复田' means 'return to farming.' Together, it means 'even if return to farming.'
  • 未必其无祸 - '未必' means 'may not,' '其' means 'it,' '无祸' means 'no disaster.' Together, it means 'may not be without disaster.'
  • 一念之善从而两家子孙皆其利泽 - '一念之善' means 'a single good thought,' '从而' means 'thereby,' '两家子孙' means 'the descendants of the two families,' '皆其利泽' means 'all benefited from it.' Together, it means 'a single good thought thereby benefited the descendants of the two families.'
  • 书曰天道福善祸淫 - '书曰' means 'the Book of History says,' '天道' means 'the way of heaven,' '福善' means 'blesses the good,' '祸淫' means 'harm the wicked.' Together, it means 'the Book of History says that the way of heaven blesses the good and harms the wicked.'
  • 又曰惟上帝不常作善降之百祥作不善降之百殃 - '又曰' means 'also says,' '惟上帝' means 'only the Supreme Deity,' '不常' means 'not always,' '作善' means 'does good,' '降之百祥' means 'bestows a hundred blessings,' '作不善' means 'does evil,' '降之百殃' means 'bestows a hundred calamities.' Together, it means 'also says that only the Supreme Deity does not always bestow a hundred blessings for good deeds and a hundred calamities for evil deeds.'
  • 呜呼天岂逺人哉 - '呜呼' means 'alas,' '天' means 'heaven,' '岂逺人哉' means 'how could it be far from people.' Together, it means 'alas, how could heaven be far from people.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 至正初元间 - Refers to the early years of the Zhi Zheng era, a period during the Yuan Dynasty.
  • 司氏 - Refers to the wife of Si, a historical figure.
  • 天道福善祸淫 - A phrase from the Book of History (Shangshu) that emphasizes the moral judgment of heaven.
  • 惟上帝不常作善降之百祥作不善降之百殃 - A phrase from the Book of History that emphasizes the moral judgment of the Supreme Deity.
  • 呜呼天岂逺人哉 - A phrase from the Book of History that emphasizes the closeness of heaven to people.
